,INT. GOLDEN SLIPPER SALOON - NIGHT.

The CAMERA OPENS ABRUPTLY on a quartet of black musicians. A tall stout woman ~ith an *impressive voice, BIG MARY, wails out a Delta blues song to her *customers from a small .stage. The. place is noisy, a large dark smokefilled room -- a World War II soldiers' bar crowded to capacity with black enlisted men in US Army uniforms. The place is jumpin'. Black couples are dancing on a postage stamp dance floor in front of the stage. Around them black soldiers and their dates are seated, crammed into small tables that cover most of the floor. Drinks and food are on the tables. Everyone is enjoying themselves. On a long mirror behind the bar~- the words: 5TH ANNIVERSARY, JULY 14, 1944, EVERYONE INVITED!. are written in broad red and yellow strokes. At the entrance, a black, muscle-bound bouncer -- balding, dressed incongrously in a tuxedo guards the door *. Black GI's stagger out of the door. The MUSIC CONTINUES. TITLES OUT. lA
EXT. STREET OUTSIDE SALOON - NIGHT

lA MASTER SERGEANT WALTERS st'Qmbles out' into the hot oppressive heat of Tynin, Louisana. He is quite drunk and continually talks and mutters to himself. The CAMERA FOLLOWS him as he pushes through the crowd of GI's and GIRLS that are hanging around outside the club. Big Mary's SONG CONTINUES OVER THE SCENE. WE TRACK with Waters as he lurches down the main street moving from the black area of town to the white area. He passes two GI's and a HOOKER. As he approaches the white hotel he refuses to step off the sidewalk for a WHITE COUPLE. As he pushes them aside the whites exchange startled looks. A HIGH WIDE SHOT as Waters turns the corner and disappears up the dark road leading out of town. A thin ground mist is hugging the road in the distance.
EXT. ROAD-* SERGEANT WATERS - NIGHT MUSIC from the saloon trails into the scene as the

CAMERA OPENS in swirling fog along a dark, moonlit bayou road, lined on both sides with swamp and moss covered trees. The night SOUNDS OF INSECTS and the STIRRING OF THE UNDERBRUSH mix with the FADING MUSIC of the saloon making the road seem eerie -- threatening. _We see a dimly lit bridge in the b.g. The CAMERA DROPS to reveal Waters in CLOSEUP. He has been beaten. A man in his S0's, streaks of blood course from his mixed-grey hair down the side of his face. Brui.ses glisten reddish and black around his eyes. Trickles of blood harden on his nose -- around his pencil-thin moustache. Dirt mixed with blood "cakes" *on his cheeks. The f cg and dim, light make him seem surreal -- a n1ghtmarish vision.
(coughing, convinced) They still hate you!

He shakes his head as if admitting a truth and looks off at someone. CLOSEUP ON WATERS (HAND HELD) As a GUN ENTERS THE FRAME. CLOSEUP - .45 CALIBER AUTOMATIC PISTOL The hammer of the pistol is pulled back. Beyond the pistol, the fog billows amidst the thick underbrush.

The pistol catches the light of the moon for an instant before the hammer powers forward, before the GUN FIRES, exploding smoke and death. Waters flies backward and over, the bullet tearing through his chest, hurting him for an instant before he dies on the dark Louisana road. His uniform is suddenly stained in a large ci~cle around the wound. As BIRDS FLUTTER through the brush. As an unseen animal disturbed by the shot SPLASHES away. The HAND HELD CAMERA MOVES TOWARD the body, stops beside it, LOOKS DOWN at it. Waters is sprawled on the road, his face still shocked in death by the vision of the person who killed him. BODY

The body jerks lifelessly as the BULLET EXPLODES in its head. There is a sudden quiet. We are aware of sudden FOOTSTEPS. They start off and fade, hardly disturbing the fog that swirls a little, then settles heavily over the body as if caressing it.
INT. BARRACKS - TAYLOR - DAY

CAMERA OPENS on the back of CAPTAIN CHARLES TAYLOR as he paces the length of a long, two-storied barracks. He is a young white officer. Dressed in fatigues and pistol belt he is tough-looking, a man of medium height, in his early thirties. He is a West Point Officer -not only spit-and-polish, but athletic*and spartanlooking with rugged though boyish good looks. On both sides of him, enli_sted men, all of whom are black stand beside their bunks, their hands over their heads as a search-team of MP's goes through the personal belongings of everyone -- beds are searched -- footlockers, walllockers, duffel bags, etc.
I'm afraid this kind of thing
arms down when the search-team finishes with you. Several men drop their arms, as Taylor passes ELLIS.
(continuing) We don't want anyone from this Fort going into Tynin looking for red-necks.
A man in his mid to late 20's, dressed in T-shirt, fatigues and, slippers steps ~orward. He is a Corporal. His name is COBB.
May I speak. sir?
Taylor nods, stops in front of the man.
(continuing) Colonel Nivens must know nobody colored killed the Sarge!
This is precautionary, Cobb
*can't ha~ the Army engaged in

Cobb steps back into place. Private First Class PETERSON clears his throat. He is bes,pectacled, young black man in his early 20's. He is tall and thin -- surprisingly even at this hour of the morning Peterson is. militarily sharp. His fatigues are pressed and starched, boots shined, brass polished, and '.as he steps forward his bearing is crisp, his manner direct.
Sir, are there any suspects?
Taylor _faces Peterson. None.
(suddenly) Everybody knows it was the Klan.

The voice is sharp. Southern and arrogant. HENSON steps forward. He is a man of medium height, stocky his face is round, his lips heavy, his forehead and eyes separated by thick, bushy eyebrows. He is dressed in a baseball shirt, cap, fatigues and boots. Taylor turns on him angrily.

Henson; unless you saw it, keep your opinions to yourself -- And that applies to everybody. else. (almost simultaneously) Yes,* sir.

But you are soldiers and you have a duty to this uniform. Your war -my war is with the Nazis and Japs, not American civilians in town -I don't care what you think they did! The town of Tynin has been placed off-limits to all enlisted personnel -- Any man found in the town will be innnediately subject to Court-Martial -- Sergeant Waters* replacement will.be assigned in a couple of weeks -- Until then, Cobb will be barracks NCO. Any questions? The men are silent.

Davenport steps *out into the glaring sun. *. We see him for the first time and we admire him immediately. He is a spit and polish black US Army officer. What attracts our attention to Davenport is his manner the boldness of it -- and flair if you will. He is wearing Gen. The uniform he's wearing has been tailored to fit him more attractively -so that while the uniform is the same, he looks like no other officer we've seen.* He attracts the attention of everyone.
